MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Feb-19-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kris M. Darrough, Esq. of PAUL PLEVIN SULLIVAN & CONNAUGHTON, T:619.237.5200, for defendant/ moving party, on Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ALLIANT INTERNATIONAL UNIVERSITY's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION as follows:

Defendant Alliant International University's petition to compel arbitration and stay is granted. The parties' separation agreement contains a broadly phrased arbitration agreement subject to the AAA Employment Arbitration Rules. AAA Rule 6 clearly and unmistakably provides that the arbitrator determines "the existence, scope or validity of the arbitration agreement." Based on that delegation clause, the arbitrator will decide whether the parties' underlying dispute is subject to arbitration. (See Dream Theater, Inc. v. Dream Theater (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 547, 557 ["The Contract provides that if a contested claim is not settled within the contractual deadline, then it must be submitted to binding arbitration in accordance with the AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules. These rules specify that the arbitrator will decide disputes over the scope of the arbitration agreement. We conclude that the parties' agreement to arbitrate according to this rule is clear and unmistakable evidence of the intent that the arbitrator will decide whether a Contested Claim is arbitrable."]; Rodriguez v. American Technologies, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 1110, 1123 [parties designated arbitrator to determine scope of arbitration by referring to AAA Construction Industry Rules].)

The court rejects plaintiff's unconscionability argument. Plaintiff does not argue that the delegation clause in the AAA rules itself, which is severable, is unconscionable. Therefore, the arbitrator is charged with determining whether the agreement to arbitrate the underlying merits is viable. (See Rent-A-Center, West, Inc. v. Jackson (2010) 561 U.S. 63, 72 [party must specifically challenge delegation clause]; Nielsen Contracting, Inc. v. Applied Underwriter, Inc. (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1096, 1108 ["[A]n argument that the arbitration agreement or the underlying contract is unenforceable is not sufficient to trigger the court's obligation to resolve contentions regarding the enforceability of a severable delegation clause."]; Aanderud v. Superior Court (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 880, 891 [party failed to show that delegation clause was substantively unconscionable].)

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
